content.title: Workflow Map
content.summary: The customer onboarding process begins after a new SaaS customer signs an agreement and is handed off from sales to customer operations. The onboarding team reviews the customer profile, confirms implementation scope, identifies integration requirements, schedules kickoff activities, and assigns internal owners.

steps.description: Sales marks the customer as closed-won and submits the onboarding handoff form.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: Customer operations reviews account details, contract notes, and implementation scope.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The onboarding coordinator checks whether required intake fields are complete.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: Missing or unclear intake information is routed back to the account executive.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The implementation specialist reviews requested integrations and technical requirements.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: Customers with sensitive data, custom integrations, or enterprise terms are routed for supervisor review.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The supervisor approves, rejects, or requests clarification on the onboarding plan.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The onboarding coordinator schedules the kickoff meeting and assigns internal owners.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The implementation specialist drafts the onboarding task plan.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: Customer success reviews customer-facing commitments before they are sent externally.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: The onboarding plan is shared with the customer.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.description: Weekly onboarding status is tracked until launch.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

approval_references.title: Process Narrative
approval_references.snippet: AI assistance may help summarize account context, identify missing intake information, suggest onboarding tasks, flag risks, and draft implementation plans. However, approval gates are required before customer-facing commitments are made, before implementation scope is changed, or before sensitive customer data is shared across systems.

approval_references.title: Policy and Controls
approval_references.snippet: Customers with sensitive data requirements, enterprise contract terms, custom integrations, or accelerated launch timelines require supervisor approval before the onboarding plan is finalized.

approval_references.title: Policy and Controls
approval_references.snippet: Write actions require explicit human approval. Audit events must capture approval, policy checks, and any project-management or customer-facing write action.

classified_fields.rationale: Synthetic/internal record identifiers are allowed in the demo context.
Customer-identifying data should not be sent to the model context.
Field name matched a conservative PII heuristic. Treat as blocked from model context until reviewed.
Field is not defined in the policy catalog and did not match a known heuristic. Defaulting to confidential and requiring review.
Field is not defined in the policy catalog and did not match a known heuristic. Defaulting to confidential and requiring review.
Field is not defined in the policy catalog and did not match a known heuristic. Defaulting to confidential and requiring review.
Field is not defined in the policy catalog and did not match a known heuristic. Defaulting to confidential and requiring review.
Field name matched an operational metadata heuristic. Treat as internal workflow data.
Field name matched a conservative PII heuristic. Treat as blocked from model context until reviewed.
Field name matched an operational metadata heuristic. Treat as internal workflow data.
Supervisor review requirement is internal control metadata.
Free-text notes may contain sensitive data and should be reviewed or redacted.
sample_values: Customer requests accelerated onboarding and SSO integration

identified_risks.risk_name: Missing or unclear onboarding intake information
identified_risks.risk_description: Onboarding plans should not be finalized when intake fields, contract context, integration details, or handoff information are missing or unclear.

identified_risks.risk_name: Incomplete intake or handoff may lead to incorrect planning
identified_risks.risk_description: Missing or unclear handoff information can cause incorrect plans, missed dependencies, or premature customer communication.

identified_risks.risk_name: Sensitive data may enter model context
identified_risks.risk_description: Fields requiring redaction or blocked from model context must be controlled before LLM use.

controls.name: Validate source and intake records before finalization
controls.description: Workflow items cannot be finalized when required source, intake, or reference information is missing or conflicting.

controls.name: Validate intake and handoff completeness
controls.description: Missing or unclear intake information must be routed for clarification before planning or customer communication.

controls.name: Human approval before backlog creation
controls.description: Backlog items may be proposed by the agent, but real issue creation requires approval.

controls.name: Audit project-management write action
controls.description: Issue creation must be logged with approval reference and created issue URL.

approval_gates.name: Sensitive-data review before model context use
approval_gates.trigger: Input includes fields blocked from model context or requiring redaction.
approval_gates.agent_allowed_action: Use approved redacted context or request data handling review
approval_gates.blocked_action_without_approval: Send sensitive or unredacted data to model context

approval_gates.blocked_action_without_approval: Advance the workflow item, finalize a decision, or communicate a final outcome

escalation_rules.name: Escalate missing or conflicting source records
escalation_rules.condition: Required source information is missing, ambiguous, or conflicts with another system or workflow record.
escalation_rules.required_action: Manual review before the workflow item can be finalized.

applies_to_steps.workflow_step: Sales marks the customer as closed-won and submits the onboarding handoff form.
The onboarding coordinator checks whether required intake fields are complete.
Missing or unclear intake information is routed back to the account executive.

escalation_rules.name: Escalate items requiring governed approval
escalation_rules.condition: Workflow item meets a policy, supervisor-review, sensitive-data, custom-scope, or elevated-risk condition.
escalation_rules.required_action: Authorized reviewer must approve, reject, or request clarification before the workflow item advances.

write_action_policy.rationale: Tool 'project_mgmt_server.create_issue' is write-capable and requires human approval.

backlog_items.title: Add sensitive-data redaction and model-context gate
backlog_items.description: Prevent fields requiring redaction or blocked from model context from being sent to the LLM without approved handling.

issue_creation_result.title: Add sensitive-data redaction and model-context gate
issue_creation_result.message: Dry run succeeded. No GitHub issue was created.

backlog_items.title: Add approval gate for high-risk onboarding plans
backlog_items.description: Require authorized review before onboarding plans involving sensitive data, custom integrations, enterprise terms, accelerated timelines, or customer-facing commitments are finalized or advanced.

backlog_items.title: Block customer-facing and system-of-record updates until approval is recorded
backlog_items.description: Keep onboarding plan updates, task assignments, customer-facing commitments, and system-of-record changes in draft state until human approval is present.

backlog_items.title: Validate onboarding intake and source records before planning
backlog_items.description: Prevent onboarding plans from being finalized when required intake fields, contract terms, integration details, or source records are missing, ambiguous, or conflicting.

backlog_items.title: Create review queue for high-risk onboarding cases
backlog_items.description: Route onboarding cases with sensitive data, custom integrations, enterprise terms, missing intake, customer-facing commitments, or compressed timelines to a review queue with required evidence and approval status.

backlog_items.title: Implement escalation rules for high-risk onboarding cases
backlog_items.description: Escalate cases that involve missing intake, sensitive customer data, custom integrations, enterprise terms, customer-facing commitments, or compressed launch timelines.

backlog_items.title: Persist approval and write-action audit events
backlog_items.description: Move audit records for approvals, policy checks, and write actions from the MVP in-memory store to a durable append-only store.
